plugin_desc: Nexans Advanced Networking Solutions (Nexans ANS) industrial managed switch-specific SNMP collection (NEXANS-BM-MIB, IANA PEN 266). In addition to standard IF-MIB interface metrics, it collects Nexans private OIDs for device health — chassis temperature (bmSwitchInfo infoTemperature, a dedicated scalar in degrees Celsius) and an industrial alarm-relay state used as a power-supply health proxy (infoAlarmStateM1). The alarm-relay raw codes (1=notSupported, 2=alarmOff which means OK, 3/7/8/9 and higher=alarm asserted including explicit power-supply S1/S2 faults) are normalized via telegraf processors.enum to 1=healthy / 2=fault, with notSupported treated as healthy. Nexans GigaSwitch/DualSwitch devices are fanless and expose no CPU, memory or fan OIDs, so those are not collected (N/A). Supported brands/models — Nexans ANS GigaSwitch / DualSwitch industrial managed switches.
name: Switch

metrics.name: device_temperature_celsius
metrics.description: This metric reports the chassis temperature in degrees Celsius of the Nexans device (NEXANS-BM-MIB bmSwitchInfo infoTemperature, a dedicated scalar typed temperature object). High temperatures can indicate cooling failure or airflow obstruction in the cabinet and risk thermal damage; the device also exposes a configured high-temperature limit (infoTemperatureMaxAllowed).

metrics.name: device_psu_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the power-supply health of the Nexans device, derived from the industrial alarm-relay output (NEXANS-BM-MIB infoAlarmStateM1). The raw codes (1=notSupported, 2=alarmOff meaning OK, 3 and higher=alarm asserted, including the explicit power-supply-S1/S2 fault codes 7/8/9) are normalized via telegraf processors.enum so that 1 = healthy and 2 = fault. A value greater than 1 indicates an asserted hardware/power alarm that warrants investigation.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCInOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifInOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCOutOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifOutOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: snmp_uptime
metrics.description: This metric indicates the uptime of the device since the last restart. By monitoring the uptime, network administrators can understand the stability and reliability of the device and identify potential reboot or failure issues.
